panel.dp1_argument: The market story is right: after ERB (retrieval-only, enterprise RAG) and the code-corpus internal matrix, "helix backs agents in non-coding environments" is the natural next chapter, and industrial asset ops is a genuinely document-hungry vertical (manuals, fault handbooks, process docs) — B is the only externally citable path to that story. But B as scoped is not "contribute scenarios following IBM's guidelines"; it is "persuade IBM to add a new axis to their benchmark." Evidence: their entire contribution surface has zero document dimension — docs/external-industrial-datasets.md maps every invited dataset to telemetry domains (iot/tsfm/vibration) with checklists about sensor naming and timestamp normalization; docs/guideline/ground_truth_design_guideline.md's "Knowledge Query" category means structured-store lookups; and the judge (src/evaluation/scorers/llm_judge.py) scores tool sequencing and asset/time/sensor accuracy while the content-grounding scorer (semantic_similarity) is NotImplementedError. So B carries three coupled upstream asks: document-grounded scenarios, a document-tool seam (DEFAULT_SERVER_PATHS in src/agent/runner.py is hardcoded to six servers), and a scoring dimension that credits context quality. The positioning payoff is real even before any score exists — "helix team contributed the document-grounded knowledge track to IBM's AssetOpsBench" is a credibility asset — but only if merged. A score on unmerged, self-authored scenarios is worth little publicly and a WEAK score on a track we authored is worse than no result. Therefore: GO as primary direction, but gate scenario-authoring effort on an upstream appetite signal, scope the pilot to one dataset-doc pairing, and separate the two public claims (contribution vs. result) so a null result cannot poison the story.
dp1_conditions: Appetite gate first: open an upstream issue/RFC (public, framed as evaluating against IBM's public benchmark and extending its invited external-dataset track) proposing the document-grounded scenario class + document MCP seam BEFORE authoring 30 scenarios; if maintainers are cold or silent for ~3-4 weeks, downgrade B to 'watch item' per the existing verdict doc.

Scoring seam is part of the contribution: propose implementing semantic_similarity (or a document-grounding judge dimension) upstream; without it, even merged scenarios structurally cannot credit helix (llm_judge.py criterion 2 is asset/time/sensor via tools).

Pilot scope = ONE pairing: CWRU bearing data + open bearing-fault/vibration-severity prose in the existing vibration domain (their vibration server and src/scenarios/local/vibration_utterance.json already exist as style anchors). No ISO 10816 text — the standard is copyrighted and non-redistributable; use open secondary sources and pass their license/provenance checklist.

Two-claim discipline: 'contributed the knowledge track' is citable at merge; 'helix result on AssetOpsBench' is citable only after merge AND at least one non-helix baseline (vanilla file-tool or naive RAG arm) runs the same scenarios, so the result is not 'we aced our own exam'.

Reshape A must run first and show the 6-dim judge separates helix-on from helix-off arms; if it does not, B's premise (a judge that rewards context quality) fails and B should be re-scoped or dropped.
panel.dp2_argument: A is the cheap experiment that answers B's two make-or-break unknowns before any public commitment: (1) does the borrowed 6-dim judge actually move when context quality changes — its rubric was built for tool orchestration, and if it cannot separate helix-attached from helix-absent arms on document-grounded questions, B's scoring premise collapses; (2) what does one document-grounded utterance + gold actually cost to author under MULTI_VALID_GOLD discipline — which calibrates B's effort budget honestly. A also fills a real internal gap (trajectory-level + hallucination scoring that the {-1,0,+1} 50-needle matrix lacks) so it has standalone value even if B dies. It needs no gandalf time (agent runner + judge are cloud calls, runnable on the dev box) and no changes to helix master. The only positioning caveat: A is explicitly not citable and must never be framed as an AssetOpsBench result — it borrows an Apache-2.0 evaluator, nothing more.
panel.dp2_minimal_scope: Smallest A that de-risks B: (1) ~10-12 document-grounded utterances over the EXISTING small fixture bed only (helix design docs + MEK specs — no new corpus work), authored to MULTI_VALID_GOLD discipline, asset-ops-STYLE phrasing; (2) attach helix MCP to the Claude SDK runner via the server_paths constructor override (src/agent/claude_agent/runner.py) — no upstream code changes; (3) two arms: helix-MCP-on vs helix-MCP-off (agent answers from parametric knowledge/no tools); (4) score both arms with their llm_judge using a non-Claude judge model (their evaluator.py rejects self-judging); (5) single deliverable: does pass-rate and/or the hallucination dimension separate the arms, plus per-scenario authoring-hours. Timebox 2-3 days, dev box only. Explicitly out of scope: xl scale curve, medium/large beds, judge prompt modifications, any public artifact.
risks: Upstream acceptance risk (B): citability is 100% gated on IBM merging a new benchmark axis they never invited — every invited contribution in their docs is telemetry-shaped; a stalled PR strands all scenario-authoring effort. Mitigate with the RFC-first appetite gate.

Self-authored-gold discount (B): helix authoring the scenarios AND being the only knowledge backend scored on them reads as 'wrote our own exam'; requires a non-helix baseline arm on the same scenarios before any result claim.

Judge insensitivity (A and B): the 6-dim rubric may not separate context-quality arms at all — data_retrieval_accuracy is defined as correct asset/time/sensor via tools, and semantic_similarity is unimplemented; A exists to test exactly this before B invests.

Weak-public-result exposure (B): a poor helix score on a track helix contributed damages the non-coding story more than silence; the two-claim discipline (contribution citable at merge, result citable only with baseline comparison) is the containment.

Corpus licensing is harder than datasets (B): the authoritative documents (ISO 10816, OEM manuals) are copyrighted/non-redistributable; usable open prose is secondary-source material, which weakens the 'ground truth requires reading the document' authority of scenarios and needs diligence their checklist demands.

Effort creep vs bench queue: both reshapes involve gold authoring; keep A off gandalf, keep hands off benchmarks/snow + #208 (Codex-owned), and hold B to the one-pairing pilot until the appetite gate passes.
panel.sequencing: This week (docs-only, matches the 0-failure-baseline preference): draft the upstream RFC text + license/provenance inventory for the CWRU-pairing pilot, and author A's 10-12 utterances + gold on paper — all documentation artifacts, no code, no gandalf. Weekend-results review stays Monday's first task untouched. A's 2-3 day run executes on the dev box after that review (cloud judge calls, zero gandalf contention). B's scenario-authoring and seam work start only after BOTH gates: A shows judge separation, and the upstream RFC gets a positive maintainer signal. This ordering means the only cost sunk before external validation is one RFC and a dozen internal utterances.
panel.killer_fact: IBM's own contribution surface contains zero document dimension — docs/external-industrial-datasets.md maps every invited public dataset to telemetry domains with checklists about sensor names and timestamps, the ground-truth guideline's 'Knowledge Query' means structured-store lookups, and the one scorer that could credit content grounding (semantic_similarity) is a NotImplementedError skeleton — so Reshape B is not 'contribute scenarios to IBM's benchmark,' it is 'convince IBM to add a new axis to their benchmark,' and the citable non-coding story exists only on the far side of that persuasion; a 2-3 day Reshape A trial tests the one assumption both reshapes share (that their judge can even see context quality) before a single public artifact is committed.

panel.dp1_argument: B is the only path to an externally citable agentic eval, and their own taxonomy invites it (utterance_design_guideline.md line 51 lists "Knowledge base and documentation lookup" under retrospective; Template 2 is "Knowledge Lookup") — but as scoped, B has a fatal epistemics hole: parametric contamination. The proposed corpora (ISO 10816 severity tables, CWRU bearing geometry, C-MAPSS papers) are famous public texts that sit in frontier-model pretraining — AssetOpsBench itself already ships ISO 10816 tables and bearing geometry as hardcoded constants, and the verdict doc (2026-07-13-assetopsbench-eval-verdict.md §2) documents that their FMSR "knowledge" path works fine on pure LLM parametric calls. A scenario whose gold is a well-known standard value does not "require reading documents" — the model answers it closed-book, helix's contribution is unfalsifiable, and any win is claim inflation. Second hole: circularity — helix's owner authoring the scenarios that helix then uniquely serves is vendor-designed gold; upstream maintainer review mitigates only if the scenarios ship with a non-helix baseline arm. Third: the 6-dim judge (llm_judge.py) still scores context only indirectly via answer text, its hallucination check is corpus-blind (judge sees question + characteristic_form + trajectory[:8000] — line 109 — and helix-injected context is exactly what an 8K cap truncates), and their external-industrial-datasets.md explicitly disclaims changing scoring or baselines, so acceptance of a doc-MCP seam + scoring change is an uncontrolled third-party timeline. B should be adopted as the primary DIRECTION but not committed as the primary WORKSTREAM until the shape conditions below pass; until then it is a feasibility-gated bet, not a plan.
dp1_conditions: Closed-book contamination gate: every candidate scenario is first run against the target model with NO corpus/tools; any utterance the model answers correctly closed-book is discarded or its gold perturbed to doc-only facts (site-specific thresholds, dataset-specific geometry, cross-document joins). This is non-negotiable — without it B measures parametric recall, not context quality.

Upstream appetite probe BEFORE authoring: open a public issue/discussion on IBM/AssetOpsBench proposing the document-grounded scenario class + generic knowledge-MCP seam (and offering to implement their NotImplementedError semantic_similarity slot as the content-grounding dimension); author the 30-scenario set only after a maintainer signals acceptance of the concept.

Baseline-arm requirement baked into the contribution: scenarios must be runnable with any document tool (filesystem/naive-RAG baseline), and any helix number is reported as helix-vs-baseline on IBM-merged gold, never helix-alone on helix-authored gold.

Licensing triage first, corpus second: ISO 10816 full text is not redistributable; start from NASA C-MAPSS papers / public-domain government docs and write the provenance note their external-industrial-datasets.md checklist demands before any utterance work.

Judge amendment or acknowledged limitation: either contribute a content-grounding dimension (their semantic_similarity skeleton) or document that the 6-dim judge credits documents only via answer correctness and the 8K trajectory cap can truncate injected context.
panel.dp2_argument: A as described is mostly busywork dressed as de-risking: B's three real risks (parametric contamination, upstream acceptance, licensing) are untouched by an internal track, and the attach mechanics A would 'validate' are already code-verified in the NO-GO doc (§4: LITELLM_BASE_URL proxy attach, server_paths override). A's numbers are triply circular — we author the questions over our own corpora, our tool serves the answers, and a borrowed LLM judge scores against our own characteristic_form — so they are not results and must never be quoted. Worse, the 6-dim rubric transfers poorly to document queries: agent_sequence_correct is vacuous with a single helix MCP server, data_retrieval_accuracy is defined as correct asset/time/sensor via tools (llm_judge.py lines 40-43) which has no referent for a design-doc question, so a naive port inflates pass rates on dimensions that can only return true. BUT a deliberately minimal A has genuine de-risking value on exactly two B-critical questions the desk research cannot answer: (1) judge-transfer audit — which of the 6 dims produce non-vacuous signal on document-grounded utterances, which is precisely what we must know before proposing scenario/scoring changes upstream; (2) contamination-control pilot — rehearse the closed-book control arm methodology on our own corpora where iteration is free. It also gives the 50-needle matrix its first trajectory-level hallucination measurement. Run it small, local, and labeled internal-only.
panel.dp2_minimal_scope: ~10 utterances (not a full track) over the existing small bed (helix design docs + MEK, already frozen per GENOME_FIXTURE_MATRIX.md — skip the small-to-xl scale curve, that is the 50-needle matrix's job), MULTI_VALID_GOLD discipline with exact-value characteristic_forms; three arms per utterance: (a) agent + helix MCP, (b) agent + naive filesystem baseline, (c) closed-book no-tools control; score all with their Apache-2.0 judge unmodified; deliverable is NOT a pass rate but a judge-transfer audit memo — per-dimension vacuous/non-vacuous classification, hallucination-dim behavior when the answer is confabulated vs doc-grounded, and how many self-authored utterances failed the closed-book gate. Runs on the local box (no gandalf time), 1-2 days, docs+bench-script only, no helix_context/ source changes.
risks: Parametric contamination: public manuals/standards gold is answerable from model memory; without the closed-book gate, B's headline number measures pretraining recall and a skeptic can zero it out with one no-tools ablation.

Circularity/claim inflation: vendor-authored scenarios where the vendor's tool is the only knowledge backend; 'external, citable' framing collapses if reviewers notice gold and backend share an author — baseline arm + maintainer-merged gold are the only defenses.

Judge-dimension vacuity: 2-3 of 6 dims (agent_sequence, asset/time/sensor retrieval accuracy) have no referent for document queries, silently inflating pass rates; hallucination dim is corpus-blind and the trajectory[:8000] cap (llm_judge.py:109) can truncate exactly the helix-injected context it should be auditing.

Upstream acceptance is an uncontrolled dependency: external-industrial-datasets.md explicitly disclaims changing scoring/baselines, DEFAULT_SERVER_PATHS is hardcoded, and the batch runner has no Claude-arm MethodConfig — 'primary direction' could stall for months on review.
Licensing hollow-out: ISO 10816 text is not redistributable; SWaT docs are request-access; the actually-shippable corpus may be much thinner than the pitch assumes.

A's internal numbers leaking into external claims ('helix passes IBM's 6-dim judge') — self-authored gold scored by a borrowed judge is not AssetOpsBench and must be labeled internal-only in the doc header.

Effort asymmetry: B = utterances + gold + upstream PR shepherding + licensing diligence + a doc-tool seam that does not exist in their harness; underestimating this while calling it 'primary' crowds out the bench queue.
panel.sequencing: This week (docs-only, gandalf occupied): (1) open the public upstream issue/discussion proposing document-grounded scenarios + knowledge-MCP seam + semantic_similarity implementation offer — zero code, starts the uncontrolled acceptance clock earliest; (2) licensing triage memo for candidate corpora. Next week, AFTER the weekend-results review (first task, per constraints) and without touching gandalf or Codex's benchmarks/snow//#208: run minimal-A locally (small bed, 3-arm, ~10 utterances). B scenario authoring begins only after both an upstream maintainer signal AND A's judge-transfer audit; if the closed-book pilot shows most public-manual facts are parametrically known, B's corpus strategy must pivot to doc-only/perturbed facts before any utterance is written.
panel.killer_fact: The corpora proposed to ground B — ISO 10816 severity tables and bearing characteristic-frequency geometry — already live inside AssetOpsBench as hardcoded constants (verdict doc §2), and its FMSR pipeline already answers 'knowledge' scenarios via pure LLM parametric calls with no retrieval at all: the benchmark is existing proof that this exact document content is answerable without reading any document, so document-grounded scenarios built on famous public manuals test model memory, not helix, unless every gold fact first fails a closed-book probe.

panel.dp1_argument: B's strategic value (an externally citable, document-grounded agentic eval where none exists) is real, but as specified B would measure approximately nothing about helix, for three compounding measurement reasons. (1) Parametric contamination: the proposed corpora — ISO 10816 severity tables, CWRU bearing-fault material, C-MAPSS papers — are canonical public texts inside every frontier model's parametric knowledge. AssetOpsBench itself already demonstrates this failure mode: its FMSR domain answers failure-mode 'knowledge' questions via hardcoded LLM prompt templates with no retrieval at all (verdict doc §2, src/servers/fmsr/main.py). Scenarios grounded in famous public standards will pass a closed-book (no-document) run, making the helix-on/helix-off delta ≈ 0. (2) The judge is grounding-blind: the 6-dim rubric's 'data_retrieval_accuracy' is defined as correct asset/time/sensor via TOOLS (llm_judge.py:40-43), 'agent_sequence_correct' rewards tool ordering, and the one scorer that could credit content grounding — semantic_similarity — is a NotImplementedError skeleton. Contributing document-grounded scenarios without also contributing a grounding/citation scorer dimension means context quality is still only scored indirectly through answer text. (3) Scale mismatch: a handful of manuals/standards per domain is a tens-to-hundreds-doc corpus; helix's demonstrated differentiators are at scale (xl rank-squeeze, 829K ERB blob, dense median-rank 384 ranking problem). In that regime naive grep or full-context stuffing ties helix, so even a successful upstream run yields no attributable helix result. B is salvageable — and worth doing as the external-facing direction — only under the shape conditions below; without the closed-book control in particular, B is NO_GO on validity grounds regardless of upstream acceptance.
dp1_conditions: Closed-book control as a gold-authoring gate: every contributed scenario must FAIL a docless frontier-model run before acceptance into the set. Pin ground truth to dataset/rig-specific values (CWRU test-rig bearing geometry, specific C-MAPSS unit/operating-condition mappings, SWaT stage-specific sensor names) rather than textbook facts — extend MULTI_VALID_GOLD's conservative-inclusion discipline with this control. This is the retrieval-science analogue of their own utterance guidelines and should be pitched upstream as methodology, not just scenarios.

Contribute a content-grounding scorer alongside scenarios — implement their semantic_similarity stub or a citation/evidence-span dimension — so the harness can directly credit context quality. Their rubric today cannot distinguish a document-grounded correct answer from a parametric one.

Corpus non-triviality floor: ship enough documents plus distractor prose that retrieval is not solved by context-stuffing; if upstream caps corpus size, reframe helix's headline metric as tokens-per-trajectory at equal pass rate (the verdict doc's option 3), which is measurable even on small corpora via the LITELLM_BASE_URL attach.
Gate the upstream scenario PR on Reshape A's judge-sensitivity result: if the 6-dim judge's score does not move between helix-on and helix-off arms internally, do not invest in upstream authoring.

Follow their external-industrial-datasets.md checklist literally (license/provenance/no-redistribution, transform-script contracts) — it is the documented acceptance path and keeps all artifacts public per the hard framing rule.
panel.dp2_argument: A first is correct sequencing from a measurement standpoint: it tests, cheaply and on our own frozen beds, the exact assumption B depends on — that their 6-dim trajectory judge produces signal correlated with retrieval quality. A also adds two things the 50-needle matrix genuinely lacks: trajectory-level scoring (does a multi-turn agent RECOVER from single-shot rank-squeeze by reformulating queries? — the xl B2 finding says single-shot rank is the ceiling; the agent-loop recovery margin is new, real IR signal) and a hallucination dimension pairable with the know/miss contract (does miss→abstain correlate with judge hallucinations=false — a natural extension of the know-confidence calibration work). But A as described is over-scoped and over-claims: (a) their hallucination check is PROCESS hallucination — 'claims success without performing the necessary actions' (llm_judge.py:61-63) — not content fabrication against the corpus, so it's narrower than the brief implies; (b) the judge truncates trajectories at 8,000 chars (llm_judge.py:109), which a multi-turn MCP trajectory with injected helix context will blow through immediately — the judge would score a truncated prefix; this must be patched or the trajectory summarized before any run is meaningful; (c) self-judging is rejected by their evaluator (evaluator.py:82-95 per verdict doc), so a Haiku agent needs a non-Claude judge, adding a cross-model judge confound; (d) at ~10-20 utterances, LLM-judge variance can swamp bed deltas, so every A utterance needs deterministic MULTI_VALID_GOLD gold_delivered as an anchor metric to validate the judge against — the judge is the thing under test, not the ground truth. Trim to the minimal scope and A is the right first move; the full 4-bed internal track is only justified after the judge shows sensitivity.
panel.dp2_minimal_scope: A judge-sensitivity probe, not a track: (1) 12-15 document-grounded utterances over the small bed corpus (helix design docs + MEK specs), authored with MULTI_VALID_GOLD discipline so each has deterministic gold_source anchors alongside a characteristic_form; (2) two beds only — small and xl — for a two-point scale contrast, not four; (3) two arms — helix MCP attached vs a no-helix baseline (filesystem grep or bare agent) — because the single decision-relevant output is whether judge score/pass separates the arms and tracks gold_delivered; (4) patch the 8K trajectory cap (or add a trajectory summarizer) and wire a non-Claude judge model before the first run; (5) report three numbers: judge-score delta helix-on vs helix-off, correlation of judge pass with deterministic gold_delivered, and trajectory-level gold recovery rate vs single-shot retrieval on the same utterances. Authoring + judge patch are desk work doable this week (docs + eval-side only, no helix pipeline changes, test baseline untouched); runs wait for the bench box.
risks: Parametric-knowledge contamination (B, fatal if unmitigated): ISO/CWRU/C-MAPSS prose is in frontier parametric knowledge; without a closed-book rejection gate the helix delta is ~0 and any published result is unattributable.

Grounding-blind scoring (both): no implemented judge dimension credits document evidence (semantic_similarity is NotImplementedError; data_retrieval_accuracy means asset/time/sensor tool correctness) — helix can only move scores indirectly via answer text.
Judge trajectory truncation at 8,000 chars (llm_judge.py:109) silently scores a prefix of any context-rich MCP trajectory; results before patching this are noise.

Hallucination dimension over-claim (A): it detects unfaithful success claims, not corpus-ungrounded content; pairing it with know/miss is valuable but should not be sold as content-faithfulness measurement.
Judge-model confound: self-judging is rejected upstream, so agent and judge are different model families; arm comparisons must hold the judge model fixed.
Small-n LLM-judge variance (A): without deterministic gold_delivered anchors per utterance, bed and arm deltas at n~15 are not distinguishable from judge noise.

Scale-regime mismatch (B): a small public-manual corpus cannot exercise helix's differentiators (sharding, rank fusion at depth, freshness); a null or tied result at that scale would be wrongly read as 'helix adds nothing'.

Upstream acceptance risk (B): scenarios + a new scorer dimension + a document-MCP seam is a large contributor ask; DEFAULT_SERVER_PATHS is hardcoded (src/agent/runner.py:16) and no config path exposes server overrides, so the seam itself is an upstream code change they must accept.

Self-authored gold circularity (A): the same team authoring utterances, gold, and characteristic_form risks writing scenarios helix is pre-shaped to win; mitigate with the closed-book control applied internally too.
panel.sequencing: This week (bench box occupied, docs-only preference): author the 12-15 A utterances + MULTI_VALID_GOLD gold, patch the judge trajectory cap in a fork, and draft the closed-book-control methodology note — all desk work, zero pipeline changes. Next week: weekend-results review first (stated first task), then run minimal A on freed bench capacity (small + xl fixtures already frozen; hands off benchmarks/snow/ and #208 per Codex ownership). B's first upstream artifact should be a docs-only scenario-design proposal PR (their invited contribution path via external-industrial-datasets.md) and is gated on A's judge-sensitivity readout — do not author the full upstream scenario set or the document-MCP seam until A shows the judge separates helix-on from helix-off.
panel.killer_fact: AssetOpsBench already contains the exact failure mode Reshape B would walk into: its FMSR 'knowledge' domain answers failure-mode questions via hardcoded LLM prompt templates drawing on parametric knowledge with zero retrieval (src/servers/fmsr/main.py, verdict doc §2) — and the judge passes those scenarios. The corpora B proposes to add (ISO 10816, CWRU, C-MAPSS) are precisely the kind of canonical public text frontier models carry parametrically, and the one scorer that could detect the difference between a grounded and a parametric answer, semantic_similarity, is an unimplemented NotImplementedError skeleton. Without a closed-book rejection gate on every scenario plus a contributed grounding scorer, B produces an 'external agentic eval' in which helix's presence is statistically invisible.

panel.dp1_argument: B as PRIMARY stacks three independent upstream acceptances on the critical path, and the live PR record says externals don't get them. (1) gh pr list on IBM/AssetOpsBench: insider PRs (ShuxinLin, ChathurangiShyalika, [maintainer]) merge same-day to 3 days; every external feature PR in the last 3 weeks is OPEN (three from yassinejebbouri since 06-27, #432 since 07-07, #406 since 06-23) or CLOSED unmerged (#404 — notably a scorer contribution). (2) B is not 'a scenario PR': document-grounded scenarios need a document-tool seam that doesn't exist (DEFAULT_SERVER_PATHS hardcoded, src/agent/runner.py:16-23), and their own external-industrial-datasets.md explicitly scopes contributions to NOT change scoring or baselines — a new knowledge domain is a category bigger than what that doc invites, and all five of their listed starter datasets are telemetry-shaped mappings into existing domains. (3) Even if scenarios merge, the 6-dim judge (llm_judge.py) scores tool sequencing and asset/time/sensor accuracy; the only scorer that would credit context quality is semantic_similarity, which is NotImplementedError — so helix's contribution to pass-rate would be indirect and plausibly null. (4) Licensing landmine sits exactly on the documents that make B document-grounded: ISO 10816 text and OEM bearing manuals are copyrighted and non-redistributable; a corpus third parties can't download is a benchmark nobody can reproduce, which destroys the 'externally citable' value that is B's entire point. (5) Optics: helix authors the scenarios, contributes the tool seam, and likely the scorer dimension, then publishes results where helix is the knowledge backend — 'vendor writes the benchmark its product wins' is the first reply any reviewer will make. B should be at most a zero-authoring probe, never this week's primary.
dp1_conditions: Zero-authoring probe FIRST: open an upstream issue tagged new-scenario (their README-invited path) proposing a document-grounded scenario class + document-tool seam; write NOTHING until a maintainer engages. Kill-switch: no substantive maintainer response in 2 weeks, B is dead.

License pre-flight before any utterance authoring: corpus restricted to public-domain/CC-BY sources (NASA C-MAPSS papers, NIST/DOE handbooks) — no ISO text, no OEM manuals. If a license-clean corpus covering >=30 scenarios cannot be listed on one page, B is dead.

Scorer condition: B only proceeds if maintainers agree a grounding/citation dimension (e.g., implementing semantic_similarity) is in scope; without it the judge cannot credit helix and the result is structurally null.

Optics condition: any published helix number requires at least one non-helix baseline arm (raw-file MCP or naive RAG) on the same scenarios, and the scenarios must be merged upstream before any helix result is cited.
DCO signoff + <300-line PRs + Conventional Commits per their CONTRIBUTING.md; budget for squash-merge review cycles.
panel.dp2_argument: A is cheap, local, Apache-2.0-clean, and adds two things the 50-needle matrix genuinely lacks (trajectory-level scoring and an explicit hallucination dimension). But be honest about what A does NOT do: it does not de-risk B's actual kill risks — upstream acceptance, licensing, and scorer coupling are untouched by an internal trial, and the attach mechanics were already verified as easy in the verdict doc (LITELLM_BASE_URL / server_paths). So approve A on its standalone merits, not as 'de-risking B'. Two changes: (1) drop the 'asset-ops-STYLE utterance' framing — styling helix-docs questions as industrial asset-ops buys zero B-credibility (self-authored gold is not AssetOpsBench either way) and adds authoring cost plus MULTI_VALID_GOLD friction; author natural doc-grounded utterances over our corpora. (2) Pre-declare the judge-mismatch null hypothesis: two of six rubric dimensions (agent_sequence_correct, data_retrieval_accuracy = correct asset/time/sensor) are asset-ops-shaped and may score degenerate on doc-QA; also their evaluator rejects self-judging (evaluator.py:82-95), so a Haiku-driven agent needs a non-Anthropic judge (local Ollama or GPT) — confirm that judge is wired before authoring 50 utterances.
panel.dp2_minimal_scope: 10-15 utterances, small bed only (helix+MEK frozen fixture), reusing existing 50-needle gold where the needle already has a doc-grounded answer. Haiku agent via claude -p with helix MCP attached; judge = one non-Anthropic model (Ollama local qualifies). Score with their Apache-2.0 llm_judge.py vendored as a script under benchmarks/ (no src/ changes, no test-suite impact, master stays at 0 failures). Hard cap: 2 days. Pre-registered kill criterion: if on the 10-utterance pilot the per-dimension judge outputs have near-zero variance (all-true or all-false on agent_sequence/data_retrieval) or unparseable-JSON rate >20%, stop — the borrowed rubric doesn't transfer and full-A is cancelled. Only if the pilot shows signal do we discuss the small-to-xl scale curve as a follow-up, and that follow-up queues BEHIND the weekend-results review and #260/#255 work.
risks: Upstream rejection/latency (HIGH): external PRs sit open 16+ days or get closed (#404) while insider PRs merge same-day. Mitigation: issue-first probe; kill-switch at 2 weeks of silence.

Benchmark-gaming optics (HIGH if published): helix authors scenarios + tool seam + scorer, then wins them. Mitigation: upstream merge before citation + mandatory non-helix baseline arm; otherwise publish nothing.

Corpus licensing (HIGH for ISO/OEM material, MEDIUM overall): ISO 10816 and manufacturer manuals are non-redistributable; non-shippable corpus kills reproducibility and thus citability. Mitigation: public-domain/CC-BY-only pre-flight before any authoring.

Scorer blindness / null result on B (HIGH): the 6-dim judge rewards tool sequencing; semantic_similarity is NotImplementedError; helix effect may be statistically invisible even on merged document scenarios. Mitigation: make a grounding dimension a precondition of B.

Judge transfer failure on A (MEDIUM): agent_sequence_correct and asset/time/sensor dimensions are meaningless for single-MCP doc-QA; risk of degenerate scores. Mitigation: 10-utterance pilot with pre-registered variance kill criterion.

Judge model availability/cost (LOW-MEDIUM): self-judging rejected (evaluator.py:82-95), so Claude-agent runs need a non-Claude judge; their stack assumes TokenRouter/watsonx. Mitigation: local Ollama judge; 1 call/scenario with 8K-char cap keeps cost trivial either way.

Roadmap displacement (MEDIUM): B-scale authoring against a 2,636-line ground-truth guideline is weeks of work competing with weekend-results review, #260 semantic-arm follow-up, #255 trim-consistency, and blend-layer retirement. Mitigation: A capped at 2 days; B gated behind the probe; gandalf and benchmarks/snow/+#208 untouched.
Checkout contention (LOW): helix main HEAD switches mid-session across concurrent agents; do any A work in a .claude/worktrees/ worktree.
panel.sequencing: This week: docs-only. (1) File the upstream probe issue (new-scenario tag) — 1 hour, zero authoring, starts B's 2-week clock without committing to B. (2) Optionally vendor llm_judge.py + write the A pilot plan doc. Next week: weekend-results review on gandalf is FIRST, untouched — A's pilot needs no gandalf time (cloud Haiku + local genome + local judge). A's 2-day pilot slots after the review and behind any #260/#255 follow-ups it competes with. B does not enter the schedule at all unless the probe gets maintainer engagement AND the license pre-flight passes; only then does a B-shaped authoring plan get written, and it must not displace blend-layer retirement.
panel.killer_fact: The live PR record contradicts B's core assumption of a welcoming upstream: gh pr list on IBM/AssetOpsBench shows insider PRs merging same-day while every external feature PR of the last three weeks is still open (three from one contributor since 06-27) or closed unmerged — including #404, an external SCORER contribution, exactly the kind of PR B would need — and B requires at least three such external acceptances (a document-tool seam their own external-datasets doc scopes out of changing scoring/baselines, the scenarios themselves, and a grounding scorer whose natural home is literally `raise NotImplementedError`) before a single helix number becomes citable.

panel.dp1_argument: B is a legitimate direction — their docs explicitly invite dataset-mapping contributor PRs (F:\Projects\AssetOpsBench\docs\external-industrial-datasets.md) and it is the only path to a citable agentic eval — but as scoped it is a multi-month, multi-PR program whose critical dependencies sit outside Max's control, so it cannot be 'primary' in any near-term planning sense without rescoping. True cost chain: (1) Corpus: the headline documents are mostly non-redistributable — ISO 10816 is a paid copyrighted standard, vendor bearing-fault handbooks (SKF/Mobius-style) are copyrighted, and their own checklist rule #1 plus 'Keep raw source data outside committed benchmark artifacts unless license allows redistribution' forbids committing them; the only clean-license prose at scale is public-domain NASA material (C-MAPSS technical reports). (2) Seam: no document tool exists — DEFAULT_SERVER_PATHS hardcodes 6 servers (src/agent/runner.py:16-23); constructors already accept a server_paths override, so helix-side experiments need NO upstream code, but making it a real upstream seam is a small PR (expose override via CLI/config) that must be accepted before any document scenario is runnable by THEIR baselines. (3) Scoring: their judge rubric (src/evaluation/scorers/llm_judge.py) credits tool sequencing and asset/time/sensor correctness, not context quality; semantic_similarity is NotImplementedError; and external-industrial-datasets.md states up front the contribution path 'does not change AssetOpsBench scoring or baseline definitions' — so B must be designed to win inside the existing rubric (characteristic_form ground truths that require document-derived facts, credited via task_completion + hallucination dims), not on a hoped-for scorer change. (4) Authoring: their ground-truth guideline is 2,636 lines and utterance guideline 584 lines — compliance is days of work per scenario batch, before upstream review latency (their repo is active, PR #445 just merged, but review cycles are weeks). (5) Gold-validity: CWRU/C-MAPSS are saturated in LLM training data, so 'ground truth requires reading the document' must be enforced with needle-grade specifics or frontier agents will pass from parametric knowledge and the helix arm shows nothing. Verdict: GO on the direction, NO on treating it as an immediate primary workstream — rescope to a staged docs-first track.
dp1_conditions: Corpus restricted to redistribution-clean sources for the first PR: NASA C-MAPSS technical reports (US-gov public domain) as the anchor; ISO 10816 and vendor handbooks may be cited/paraphrased in ground truth but never committed as corpus text

First upstream artifact is a DOCS-ONLY mapping PR following their own 9-step checklist steps 1-2 (license/provenance + mapping table) — this fits helix's docs-only-week constraint and tests maintainer appetite before any code or scenario investment

Executable-scenario PR and the server_paths-CLI-exposure seam PR are gated on maintainer engagement with the mapping PR; no scenario authoring at the 2,636-line-guideline compliance level until that signal exists

Do NOT take a dependency on their scoring changing (semantic_similarity stays unimplemented in planning assumptions); design characteristic_form so document-reading is credited through the existing task_completion / data_retrieval_accuracy / hallucination dimensions

Every utterance must pass a parametric-leakage check: a no-tools direct_llm baseline run must FAIL it before it counts as document-grounded (their direct_llm MethodConfig in src/benchmark/scenario_suite_runner.py is the free control arm)
Framing in all public artifacts: evaluating against IBM's public benchmark, per the hard rule
panel.dp2_argument: A is cheap because everything expensive about AssetOpsBench is skippable: we do not need their scenarios, servers, CouchDB, or uv harness — only the judge. Their evaluator layer is cleanly vendorable Apache-2.0 code: llm_judge.py is 173 lines against a one-method LLMBackend interface (single generate() call), Evaluator is 124 lines, and LLMJudgeScorer can be called directly with a constructed Scenario (text + characteristic_form) plus answer and trajectory strings — no PersistedTrajectory plumbing required. The agent loop is already built: bench_claude_matrix.py runs claude -p with helix MCP attached, manages fixture swap via BenchServer, and captures answers; the only new mechanics are (a) trajectory capture (claude -p stream-json or SDK verbose output, truncated to their 8,000-char judge cap) and (b) a judge backend shim honoring the self-judging guard (evaluator.py:82-95 — id-based, so use a non-Anthropic judge or explicitly accept the different-Claude-model-id loophole and document it). No CouchDB, no Docker, no uv: A runs entirely on the Windows dev box against the small fixture, leaving gandalf untouched. The dominant cost is utterance+gold authoring under MULTI_VALID_GOLD — the same cost class as prior 50-needle authoring, so a 20-utterance slice is 1-2 days. Honest caveat baked into the GO: A de-risks B's evaluator wiring, trajectory capture, hallucination-dimension scoring, and authoring-cost estimate, but it does NOT de-risk B's real blockers (corpus licensing, upstream acceptance) — those are only de-risked by B's own docs-only mapping PR, which should run in parallel. A also delivers standalone value the internal matrix lacks: trajectory-level hallucination scoring versus the current {-1,0,+1} answer scoring.
panel.dp2_minimal_scope: One week-slice, Windows dev box only, no bench-box time: (1) vendor llm_judge.py + minimal Scorer/Scenario dataclasses into benchmarks/assetops_judge/ with provenance header (Apache-2.0, upstream commit f855d4a) and a ~50-line LLMBackend shim over a non-Claude judge endpoint; (2) author 20 asset-ops-style utterances (10 helix design docs, 10 MEK specs) with MULTI_VALID_GOLD gold + characteristic_form, each passing a no-MCP claude -p control (agent must fail without helix context); (3) extend bench_claude_matrix.py's run_claude to also persist the tool-call trajectory (stream-json), 8K-char cap; (4) run small bed only — NO scale curve to xl in the trial (that doubles run cost for no de-risking value); (5) one results doc comparing 6-dim judge verdicts vs existing {-1,0,+1} scores on the same 20 answers, with explicit per-dimension agreement table. Success criterion: judge wiring produces parseable, stable verdicts and the hallucination dimension discriminates at least one case the substring scorer missed. Total: ~2-3 days plus authoring.
risks: Corpus licensing (B): ISO 10816 and vendor bearing handbooks are copyrighted and non-redistributable under IBM's own contribution rules — the clean corpus may shrink to NASA public-domain material only, weakening the 'documents pair with datasets' premise

Parametric leakage (A and B): CWRU/C-MAPSS/bearing-fault physics saturate LLM training data; without a no-tools control arm per utterance, 'document-grounded' gold silently degrades to parametric-knowledge questions and the helix arm measures nothing

Upstream acceptance latency (B): a 3-PR chain (mapping docs, server_paths seam, scenarios) through an external review process makes B's timeline uncontrollable; treating it as 'primary' invites months of idle dependency

Scorer mismatch (B): the existing 6-dim rubric credits tool sequencing; even accepted document scenarios give helix only indirect credit unless characteristic_form is engineered to require document facts — and their docs declare scoring changes out of scope

Judge dependency (A): self-judging guard (evaluator.py:82-95) is model-id-based only; using a sibling Claude model as judge technically passes but is scientifically weak — a non-Anthropic judge adds a cross-provider key/cost dependency

Scope creep (A): self-authored gold + a new judge dimension invites expanding to the full 4-bed scale curve and 50+ utterances; the trial's value is mechanics validation, capped at 20 utterances/1 bed

Windows friction is confined to B's stock harness (uv + Docker CouchDB per-scenario reset in scenario_suite_runner.py); A avoids it entirely, but any future stock AssetOpsBench baseline run belongs on the Linux bench box
A's results are not externally citable and must not be presented as AssetOpsBench numbers — different scenarios, different corpus, borrowed rubric only
panel.sequencing: Week of 07-13 (bench box occupied, docs-only preference): Day 1-2 in parallel — (a) B step-0: licensing audit + draft the upstream docs-only mapping PR (pure docs, satisfies this week's constraint, starts the maintainer-signal clock, zero gandalf time); (b) A: vendor the judge + author the 20 utterances (authoring is keyboard work, no bench runs). Day 3-4: A's local run on the Windows dev box against the small fixture — this never touches gandalf, so it does not conflict with the weekend-results review, which stays next week's first task. Week 2, after weekend review: read A's judge-vs-substring agreement doc, then make the B executable-scenario commitment decision with (1) validated evaluator mechanics, (2) a measured per-utterance authoring cost, and (3) whatever maintainer response the mapping PR drew. benchmarks/snow/ and #208 untouched (Codex-owned). Net: A first as a trial is correct sequencing, but B's docs-only step-0 should NOT wait behind A — it is the only action that de-risks B's true blockers and it costs nothing this week.
panel.killer_fact: IBM's own contribution page (docs/external-industrial-datasets.md) opens by declaring it 'does not change AssetOpsBench scoring or baseline definitions' — while the only scorer that could ever credit context quality (semantic_similarity) is a NotImplementedError skeleton and the live 6-dim judge (src/evaluation/scorers/llm_judge.py) passes/fails on tool sequencing and asset/time/sensor correctness. So the invited upstream path structurally cannot score what helix is for: B can land perfectly and still be graded by a rubric that only credits helix indirectly, which is why B must be rescoped as a staged, signal-gated track rather than the primary bet, and why the cheap move this week is A (vendor the 173-line judge, skip CouchDB/uv entirely, reuse bench_claude_matrix.py) plus B's zero-cost docs-only mapping PR.

panel.dp1_argument: The upstream door is genuinely open, but only for one of B's three components — and the ecosystem's own history shows where the other two end up. What's invited: F:\Projects\AssetOpsBench\README.md ("Call for Scenario Contribution", lines 269-284) explicitly solicits scenario PRs or `new-scenario`-tagged issues via the public repo, and docs/external-industrial-datasets.md is a maintainer-authored on-ramp for exactly this kind of dataset+scenario mapping (with a 9-step contract at lines 91-104). The repo is highly active (PR #445 squash-merged 2026-07-13, the day of this council; KDD 2026 tutorial upcoming), so review responsiveness is plausible. What's NOT precedented: the README's "University Projects & Extensions" section (lines 248-266) lists ~15 knowledge/tooling extensions — including a Columbia team's "Knowledge Plugin Server that enables injection of context-specific documentation" (line 260), which is literally B's proposed document-tool seam — and every single one lives as a fork or external repo listed in the README, none merged into core. A helix "knowledge MCP server" seam PR therefore has fork-listing as its empirically likely endpoint, not core merge; CONTRIBUTING.md's <300-changed-lines preference (line 9) and DCO/squash discipline make a harness+scorer+scenario campaign a multi-PR, multi-month effort whose latency Max does not control. The scoring problem compounds this: their judge (src/evaluation/scorers/llm_judge.py) credits context quality only indirectly via task-completion and the hallucination dimension, and the natural home for grounding — semantic.py — is a NotImplementedError stub (line 16), so B's payoff depends on authoring gold that fails without documents. And their own FMSR design (failure-mode reasoning answered by hardcoded parametric prompt templates, per the verdict doc) is evidence that public industrial-domain knowledge sits in frontier-model weights — doc-grounded gold over public manuals must beat parametric priors, which is the hardest authoring problem in this plan. B is right as the strategic DIRECTION (it is the only path to an externally citable agentic eval), but it must be reshaped issue-first, licensing-first, and gated on a maintainer signal before any large authoring investment.
dp1_conditions: Issue-first gate: open a `new-scenario`-tagged issue on IBM/AssetOpsBench proposing the document-grounded scenario track + a generic document/knowledge server type BEFORE authoring scenarios; treat explicit maintainer engagement (their README names this exact channel, lines 281-284) as the gate for all further B investment. This is docs-only and can happen this week.

Corpus licensing rule: only verifiably redistributable prose enters scenario fixtures — public-domain US-government works (NASA C-MAPSS reports), CC-BY papers, and self-authored paraphrases with provenance metadata. ISO 10816 text (paywalled) and vendor bearing handbooks (SKF/Mobius-class, copyrighted) may only appear as original summaries; this follows their own checklist (external-industrial-datasets.md lines 9-14, 108: 'Keep raw source data outside committed benchmark artifacts unless license allows redistribution').

Seam PR must be helix-agnostic and small: expose the existing `server_paths` constructor override (src/agent/runner.py:38) via CLI/config plus a generic 'knowledge' entry — under 300 lines, no helix reference in upstream code; helix attaches behind the seam as one of many possible backends.

Anti-parametric gold discipline: every contributed utterance must ship with a no-retrieval baseline result showing the answer is NOT recoverable from model weights (doc-specific values, report-specific tables), merging MULTI_VALID_GOLD discipline with their ground_truth_design_guideline.md five-phase workflow.

Time-box and honest downgrade: if no maintainer engagement within ~3 weeks of the issue, B degrades to 'helix fork + README University-Extensions listing' — still useful, but stop describing it as an externally citable IBM-benchmark result.
panel.dp2_argument: A should run first, but smaller than described. A de-risks the two B unknowns that the issue-first move cannot: (1) can we author document-grounded utterances whose gold defeats parametric priors, and (2) does their 6-dim judge actually discriminate helix-on vs helix-off trajectories (the hallucination dimension in llm_judge.py:30,63,137-140 is the capability our 50-needle {-1,0,+1} matrix lacks). Both answers become evidence attached to the upstream proposal — a B pitch that arrives with "we validated your judge discriminates document-grounding on N scenarios" is categorically stronger than a bare idea, and matches how every listed University extension earned its README slot (working repo + results). Apache-2.0 makes borrowing llm_judge.py clean with attribution. Cut from the described A: the small-to-xl scale curve (that is a post-trial study, not de-risking), and their entire harness/CouchDB stack (the verdict doc §4 shows the Claude SDK arm needs a MethodConfig addition anyway — borrow the judge, not the harness). One hard constraint from their own code: the evaluator rejects self-judging (src/evaluation/evaluator.py:82-95), so a Claude-driven agent needs a non-Claude judge model. A is NOT externally citable and must never be framed as an AssetOpsBench result — it is a judge-transplant trial.
panel.dp2_minimal_scope: Borrow exactly one file: src/evaluation/scorers/llm_judge.py (Apache-2.0, retain license/attribution notice). Author 10-15 asset-ops-style, document-grounded utterances over the SMALL bed only (helix design docs + MEK specs) under MULTI_VALID_GOLD discipline, each with a written characteristic_form per their utterance schema. Agent = `claude -p` (Haiku default per bench convention) with helix MCP attached; two arms: helix-on vs helix-off (no retrieval). Judge = a non-Claude model (local Qwen/Llama via Ollama) to respect their self-judging rejection. Success criteria: (a) judge pass-rate delta between arms >0 with the hallucination dimension firing on the helix-off arm, and (b) at least 10/15 utterances confirmed parametric-proof (helix-off fails them). Runs on local hardware or after gandalf frees — not on the bench box this week, and nowhere near benchmarks/snow/ (#208, Codex-owned). Output is one verdict doc plus the evidence bundle for the upstream issue.
risks: Fork-listing endpoint: all ~15 ecosystem knowledge/tooling extensions in README lines 248-266 live as forks/external repos, none merged to core — B's document-tool seam most likely ends as a README listing, which is weaker than 'evaluated against IBM's public benchmark' and must not be oversold.

Parametric leakage: public bearing-fault/ISO/C-MAPSS knowledge is in frontier-model weights (their FMSR domain answers 'knowledge' queries with hardcoded parametric prompt templates) — doc-grounded gold that genuinely requires retrieval may prove unauthorable at scale over redistributable public texts, nulling helix's measurable contribution.

Licensing squeeze: the most authoritative corpus texts (ISO 10816, vendor handbooks) are non-redistributable; the merge-eligible corpus shrinks to public-domain/CC material, which is both lower-value and higher parametric-leakage.

Scorer blindness: semantic.py and code_based.py are NotImplementedError stubs; context quality is scored only indirectly, so even accepted scenarios credit helix only through completion/hallucination deltas.

Review-latency dependency: CONTRIBUTING.md <300-line PRs + DCO + squash means B is a multi-PR campaign on IBM's calendar (KDD 2026 prep may consume maintainer attention in Aug); helix roadmap items must not block on it.
Contamination risk: A uses self-authored gold with their judge — any public write-up that blurs A results into 'AssetOpsBench performance' damages credibility and the upstream relationship.

Judge-model validity: A requires a non-Claude judge (evaluator.py self-judging rejection); a weak local judge may produce noisy 6-dim scores, so the arm-delta criterion needs a judge-sanity check first.
panel.sequencing: This week (docs-only, matches the 0-failure/docs-preferred constraint): (1) draft and open the upstream `new-scenario` issue proposing the document-grounded track + generic knowledge-server seam, citing their external-industrial-datasets.md checklist; (2) write the corpus licensing survey (which texts are redistributable vs summary-only). Next week: weekend-results review on gandalf FIRST as already committed, then run minimal A (judge-transplant trial) on freed or local hardware — it does not need the bench box's genome fixtures beyond the small bed. B authoring investment starts only when BOTH gates pass: maintainer engagement on the issue AND A shows a positive helix-on/off judge delta with parametric-proof gold. Hands off benchmarks/snow/ and #208 (Codex) throughout. If either gate fails, fall back to the fork + README-listing shape and re-scope B's claim accordingly.
panel.killer_fact: B's proposed document-tool seam already exists in the AssetOpsBench ecosystem — a Columbia team's "Knowledge Plugin Server that enables injection of context-specific documentation" (README.md line 260) — and like every one of the ~15 knowledge/tooling extensions in that section, it lives as a fork listed in the README, not merged core code: the empirical precedent for B's endgame is a fork listing, while the corpus texts authoritative enough to ground scenarios (ISO 10816, vendor handbooks) are the exact ones their own checklist forbids redistributing.

chair.vote_tally: DP1: 5 GO_WITH_CHANGES, 1 NO_GO (red-team). DP2: 2 GO (product-positioning, engineering-cost), 4 GO_WITH_CHANGES (bench-epistemics, retrieval-science, oss-upstream-strategy, red-team). Chair rulings: DP1 GO_WITH_CHANGES (as gated direction, not committed workstream), DP2 GO_WITH_CHANGES (operative).
binding_conditions: B-1 (appetite gate): File a public issue tagged `new-scenario` on IBM/AssetOpsBench this week proposing the document-grounded scenario class + a generic knowledge-server seam (exposing the existing server_paths constructor override, src/agent/runner.py:38). No scenario authoring before substantive maintainer engagement. Kill-switch: no engagement within 3 weeks, B downgrades to fork + README University-Extensions listing and all 'externally citable IBM benchmark' framing is retired.

B-2 (licensing pre-flight): One-page redistribution-clean corpus inventory BEFORE any utterance work. Public-domain (NASA C-MAPSS reports) and CC-BY sources only; ISO 10816 text and OEM/vendor handbooks may be paraphrased in gold but never committed as corpus, per their own checklist in docs/external-industrial-datasets.md. If a clean corpus covering ~30 scenarios cannot fit on one page, B is dead.

B-3 (closed-book contamination gate): Every candidate B scenario must FAIL a no-tools frontier-model run before entering the set; gold pinned to dataset/rig/report-specific values, not textbook facts. Non-negotiable.

B-4 (two-claim discipline + baseline arm): 'Contributed the document-grounded track' is citable at upstream merge; a helix RESULT is citable only after merge AND a non-helix baseline arm (filesystem/naive-RAG) runs the same scenarios. No helix-alone numbers on helix-authored gold, ever.

B-5 (no scoring-change dependency): Plan assumes semantic_similarity stays NotImplementedError and the 6-dim judge is what it is; offering to implement a grounding scorer is permitted but nothing in B's timeline or claims may depend on its acceptance (their external-datasets doc explicitly scopes scoring changes out).

A-1 (scope caps): <=15 utterances, small bed only (helix design docs + MEK), three arms per utterance — helix MCP on / naive filesystem baseline / closed-book no-tools control — each utterance carrying deterministic MULTI_VALID_GOLD anchors alongside its characteristic_form. No xl scale curve in the trial.

A-2 (judge mechanics): Vendor src/evaluation/scorers/llm_judge.py into benchmarks/assetops_judge/ with Apache-2.0 attribution + upstream commit pin; judge model must be non-Claude (evaluator.py:82-95 self-judging guard; the sibling-Claude-id loophole is not to be used); trajectory passed to the judge must be a structured digest <=8,000 chars or the cap raised in the vendored copy with the deviation documented (llm_judge.py:109).

A-3 (pre-registered kill criterion): If agent_sequence_correct / data_retrieval_accuracy show near-zero variance (degenerate all-true/all-false) or unparseable-JSON rate >20% on the first ~10 utterances, stop — the rubric does not transfer, full-A is cancelled, and B's scoring premise is re-examined before any upstream authoring.

A-4 (deliverable + labeling): Output is a judge-transfer audit memo (per-dimension vacuity classification, hallucination-dim behavior on confabulated vs grounded answers, closed-book failure count, judge-vs-{-1,0,+1} agreement table), NOT a pass rate. Header marked internal-only; never framed as an AssetOpsBench result.

A-5 (footprint): Dev box only, 2-3 day timebox plus authoring; zero gandalf time; no helix_context/ source changes (master stays at 0 test failures); benchmarks/snow/ and #208 untouched (Codex-owned); use a .claude/worktrees/ worktree.
SEQ-1: Weekend-results review remains next week's first task. B scenario authoring begins only after B-1 AND B-2 pass AND A's audit shows the judge separates helix-on from helix-off arms.
chair.first_slice: This week, docs-only, in parallel: (a) file the upstream `new-scenario` issue on IBM/AssetOpsBench proposing the document-grounded scenario class + generic knowledge-server seam, citing their external-industrial-datasets.md checklist — ~1 hour, zero authoring, starts the 3-week appetite clock; (b) write the one-page corpus licensing inventory (public-domain/CC-BY only); (c) desk-author the 10-15 A utterances with MULTI_VALID_GOLD gold + characteristic_forms and vendor llm_judge.py with attribution into benchmarks/assetops_judge/. A's 2-3 day run executes on the dev box next week, after the weekend-results review.
dissents: red-team dissents on DP1 (NO_GO): its PR-record evidence was chair-verified and is real — insider PRs merge same-day to 3 days while external feature PRs sit open 16+ days (#414/#415/#416, #432, #406) and #404, an external scorer contribution, closed unmerged; all ~15 knowledge/tooling extensions live as forks, none merged to core. The chair absorbs this via the 3-week kill-switch and the explicit fork+README-listing downgrade path rather than killing B outright, partly because the verification also showed the downgrade path is fast and open (external README-listing PR #419 merged in under 2 hours). Red-team would still not call B 'primary'; the chair's ruling makes 'primary' conditional enough that the disagreement is mostly about labeling, and it is recorded.

retrieval-science dissents on A's bed scope: it wanted a small+xl two-point scale contrast in the trial. Overruled to small-only (bench-epistemics, oss-upstream, engineering-cost, red-team all cut the curve) — scale contrast is the 50-needle matrix's job and doubles run cost without de-risking B.

product-positioning and engineering-cost voted unconditional GO on DP2; the chair tightened to GO_WITH_CHANGES by making red-team's variance/parse kill criterion and the 8K-cap handling binding rather than advisory.

red-team also dissents on A's purpose: it holds that A does not de-risk B's real blockers (upstream acceptance, licensing) and should be approved on standalone merits only. The chair partially agrees — A de-risks judge transfer, contamination-control methodology, and authoring cost, while B's true blockers are de-risked by the parallel issue + licensing slice — and the sequencing reflects that split.
chair.synthesis_md:
## Chair verdict

Both reshapes proceed, neither as pitched. **DP1: GO_WITH_CHANGES** — B is adopted as the primary *direction* (it is the only path to an externally citable, document-grounded agentic eval), but not as a committed workstream: zero scenario authoring until an upstream appetite signal and a licensing pre-flight both pass, with a 3-week kill-switch that downgrades B to a fork + README-listing. **DP2: GO_WITH_CHANGES** (operative, owner pre-approved contingent on this council) — A runs first as a deliberately minimal *judge-transfer trial*, not an internal track: ~10-15 utterances, small bed, three arms, vendored Apache-2.0 judge, pre-registered kill criteria, internal-only labeling. Every load-bearing code citation from the panels was chair-verified against the clone (`trajectory_text[:8000]` at llm_judge.py:109; `semantic_similarity` = `NotImplementedError`; self-judging rejection at evaluator.py:82-95, model-id based; hardcoded `DEFAULT_SERVER_PATHS` with a `server_paths` constructor override at runner.py:16-43; the external-datasets doc's "does not change AssetOpsBench scoring or baseline definitions" disclaimer; the Columbia Knowledge Plugin Server listed as an external repo). Red-team's PR-record claim was verified live via `gh pr list` and is accurate, with one counter-nuance the chair adds: external README-listing PR #419 merged in under 2 hours, so the downgrade path is demonstrably viable.

## DP1 — Reshape B as primary direction: GO_WITH_CHANGES

**The case for.** The upstream door is genuinely open for scenarios: README's "Call for Scenario Contribution" invites PRs or `new-scenario`-tagged issues with named maintainer contacts, and docs/external-industrial-datasets.md is a maintainer-authored on-ramp with a 9-step contract. The repo is highly active (PR #447 merged the day of this council). Product-positioning is right that "helix backs agents in non-coding environments" is the natural next chapter after ERB, and that a merged contribution is a credibility asset *before any score exists*. Five of six lenses converged independently on the same shape: direction yes, commitment gated.

**The case against — all of it survives scrutiny.** Three verified structural problems mean B-as-pitched would measure approximately nothing:

1. **Parametric contamination** (bench-epistemics, retrieval-science): "the benchmark is existing proof that this exact document content is answerable without reading any document" — AssetOpsBench's own FMSR domain answers "knowledge" scenarios via hardcoded parametric prompt templates with zero retrieval, and ships ISO 10816 tables and bearing geometry as constants. Famous public manuals test model memory, not helix.
2. **Grounding-blind scoring** (engineering-cost's killer fact): the contribution page opens by declaring it "does not change AssetOpsBench scoring or baseline definitions," while the only scorer that could credit context quality (`semantic_similarity`) is a `NotImplementedError` skeleton and the live 6-dim judge passes/fails on tool sequencing and asset/time/sensor correctness (llm_judge.py:40-43). The invited path structurally cannot score what helix is for.
3. **Acceptance asymmetry** (red-team, chair-verified): insider PRs merge same-day to 3 days; every external feature PR of the last three weeks is open or closed unmerged — including #404, an external *scorer* contribution. And oss-upstream's killer fact: B's document-tool seam already exists in the ecosystem as a Columbia team's "Knowledge Plugin Server" (README line 260) — living, like all ~15 knowledge/tooling extensions, as a fork listing, never merged core. The empirical endpoint of B's seam is a README listing.

Plus the **licensing squeeze**: ISO 10816 text and OEM handbooks are non-redistributable under IBM's own checklist; the clean corpus shrinks toward public-domain NASA material, which is also the most parametrically leaked.

**Resolution.** The gap between the five GO_WITH_CHANGES votes and red-team's NO_GO is smaller than it looks: nobody endorsed B as an ungated near-term primary workstream. The chair rules B the primary *direction* with hard gates (conditions B-1 through B-5) and an honest downgrade: if maintainers don't engage within 3 weeks, or the licensing one-pager comes up empty, B becomes "helix fork + README University-Extensions listing" — which the PR record shows merges fast — and the "externally citable IBM benchmark result" framing is retired. Everything public is framed as evaluating against IBM's public benchmark, per the hard rule.

## DP2 — Reshape A as first trial: GO_WITH_CHANGES

**What A actually de-risks — and what it doesn't.** Red-team's caution is adopted as ground truth: A does *not* touch B's kill risks (upstream acceptance, licensing) — those are de-risked only by the parallel issue + licensing slice. What A genuinely answers, cheaply and before any public artifact: (1) **judge transfer** — do the 6 dims produce non-vacuous signal on document-grounded queries? `agent_sequence_correct` is near-vacuous with a single MCP server and `data_retrieval_accuracy` is defined as correct asset/time/sensor via tools, so a naive port inflates pass rates on dimensions that can only return true; (2) **contamination-control rehearsal** — the closed-book gate methodology B depends on, iterated where it's free; (3) **authoring cost calibration** for B's effort budget. Standalone value even if B dies: the 50-needle matrix's first trajectory-level and hallucination-dimension measurement — with retrieval-science's honest caveat that the hallucination check is *process* hallucination ("claims success without performing the necessary actions," llm_judge.py:61-63), not content faithfulness, and must not be oversold.

**Mechanics are cheap and confirmed.** Engineering-cost is correct: vendor one 173-line Apache-2.0 file, skip CouchDB/uv/Docker entirely, reuse the existing `claude -p` + helix MCP bench loop. Two hard mechanical constraints are binding: a non-Claude judge (evaluator.py:82-95 rejects self-judging by model id; the sibling-Claude loophole is documented and not used), and the 8K trajectory cap (llm_judge.py:109) — a context-rich MCP trajectory blows through it immediately, so the judge input must be a structured digest or the vendored cap raised with the deviation noted.

**Minimal scope (binding).** 10-15 asset-ops-style, document-grounded utterances over the small bed only (helix design docs + MEK, already frozen), MULTI_VALID_GOLD discipline with deterministic gold anchors per utterance (the judge is the thing under test — it needs a ground-truth yardstick, per retrieval-science). Three arms per bench-epistemics: helix MCP on / naive filesystem baseline / closed-book no-tools control. Deliverable is a **judge-transfer audit memo**, not a pass rate: per-dimension vacuity table, hallucination-dim behavior, closed-book failure count, agreement vs the {-1,0,+1} scorer. Pre-registered kill criterion (red-team): degenerate variance on the asset-ops-shaped dims or >20% unparseable JSON → stop, full-A cancelled, B's scoring premise re-examined. Dev box only, 2-3 days plus authoring, no gandalf, no helix_context/ changes, internal-only header.

## Binding conditions

See structured list: B-1 appetite gate (issue-first, 3-week kill-switch), B-2 licensing pre-flight (one page, PD/CC-BY only), B-3 closed-book contamination gate on every scenario, B-4 two-claim discipline + mandatory non-helix baseline arm, B-5 no dependency on upstream scoring changes; A-1 scope caps (≤15 utterances, small bed, 3 arms), A-2 judge mechanics (attribution, non-Claude judge, 8K-cap handling), A-3 pre-registered kill criterion, A-4 audit-memo deliverable labeled internal-only, A-5 footprint (dev box, 0-failure master, Codex territory untouched); SEQ-1 weekend review first, B authoring gated on B-1 ∧ B-2 ∧ A's audit.

## First slice

This week, docs-only, in parallel: (a) file the upstream `new-scenario` issue proposing the document-grounded scenario class + generic knowledge-server seam — ~1 hour, starts the appetite clock; (b) the corpus licensing one-pager; (c) desk-author A's utterances + gold and vendor llm_judge.py into `benchmarks/assetops_judge/`. A runs on the dev box next week after the weekend-results review.

## Dissents

- **red-team (DP1 NO_GO):** verified evidence, honestly recorded — external feature PRs languish while insiders merge same-day, and #404 (an external scorer PR, exactly what B needs) closed unmerged. Absorbed via the kill-switch + downgrade path rather than a kill; the chair's verification added that the downgrade path itself (README listing, PR #419) merges in hours. Residual disagreement is about whether a gated direction deserves the word "primary."
- **retrieval-science (A bed scope):** wanted small+xl two-point contrast; overruled to small-only — scale curves are the 50-needle matrix's job, not trial de-risking.
- **product-positioning / engineering-cost (DP2 GO):** tightened to GO_WITH_CHANGES by making the kill criterion and 8K-cap handling binding.
- **red-team (A's purpose):** A approved on standalone merits, not as full B de-risking — the chair splits the difference: A de-risks judge transfer + contamination methodology + authoring cost; the issue + licensing slice de-risks the rest, in parallel.

## Risks register

| Risk | Sev | Owner reshape | Containment |
|---|---|---|---|
| Parametric contamination — public-manual gold answerable closed-book; helix delta ≈ 0 | HIGH | B (and A) | B-3 closed-book gate on every scenario; A rehearses the methodology |
| Grounding-blind judge — semantic_similarity unimplemented; 6-dim rubric credits tool sequencing | HIGH | B | B-5: no scoring-change dependency; design characteristic_forms to require doc facts; A-3 tests transfer first |
| Upstream acceptance latency/rejection — external PRs languish; seam + scenarios + scorer = 3 acceptances | HIGH | B | B-1 issue-first + 3-week kill-switch + fork/README downgrade |
| Licensing hollow-out — ISO/OEM texts non-redistributable | HIGH | B | B-2 pre-flight; PD/CC-BY only; kill if corpus won't fill a page |
| Vendor-writes-its-own-exam optics | HIGH if published | B | B-4 baseline arm + merge-before-citation + two-claim discipline |
| Judge-dimension vacuity inflating A pass rates | MED | A | A-3 pre-registered variance kill criterion; audit-memo (not pass-rate) deliverable |
| 8K trajectory truncation silently scoring a prefix | MED | A | A-2 digest-or-documented-cap-raise before first run |
| Hallucination-dim over-claim (process, not content faithfulness) | MED | A | Explicit caveat in the audit memo; never sold as faithfulness measurement |
| A results leaking into external claims | MED | A | A-4 internal-only header; never framed as AssetOpsBench numbers |
| Roadmap displacement vs weekend review, #260/#255, blend-layer retirement | MED | both | A timeboxed 2-3 days; B enters the schedule only after gates; gandalf + benchmarks/snow/ + #208 untouched |
| Checkout contention on helix main | LOW | A | Work in a .claude/worktrees/ worktree |

**Surviving killer facts (chair-verified):** (1) The corpora proposed to ground B already live inside AssetOpsBench as hardcoded constants, and its FMSR pipeline answers "knowledge" scenarios via pure parametric LLM calls — the benchmark itself proves this content is answerable without reading documents. (2) The invited contribution path declares scoring/baselines out of scope while the only content-grounding scorer is `raise NotImplementedError` — the rubric structurally cannot see what helix is for. (3) The live PR record shows external feature and scorer PRs do not merge, while B's proposed seam already exists in the ecosystem as an unmerged fork listing — making the fork+README endpoint, not core merge, the empirical prior B must plan around.
